---
title: While remote work surged post-pandemic, Rural-Nonrural gap emerged
description: County-level density distributions of workforce shares working from home,
  comparing 2018 and 2023 for rural and nonrural counties, with IQR outliers removed.
date: '2026-05-07'
layout: default
permalink: /charts/rural-economic-outlook-3-6-remote-work-density-no-outliers/
categories: []
tags: []
image: 3.6_remote_work_density_no_outliers.svg
chart:
  title: While remote work surged post-pandemic, Rural-Nonrural gap emerged
  chartType: density
  interactive: false
  dataSource: CORI analysis
  sourceUrl: https://github.com/ruralinnovation/data-rural-economic-outlook
  featured: false
producedBy: rural-economic-outlook
usesDatasets:
  - american-community-survey
usesPackages:
  - ruraldefinitions
usesResources: []
format:
  gfm:
    toc: false
    wrap: none
execute:
  echo: false
  warning: false
  message: false
---

![Overlays density distributions of county-level remote work shares for rural and nonrural counties in 2018 (pre-pandemic) and 2023 (post-pandemic), using ACS 5-year estimates with outliers removed via IQR method.](./3.6_remote_work_density_no_outliers.svg)

## Overview

Overlays density distributions of county-level remote work shares for rural and nonrural counties in 2018 (pre-pandemic) and 2023 (post-pandemic), using ACS 5-year estimates with outliers removed via IQR method.

## Key Findings

- Remote work shares rose dramatically from 2018 to 2023 in both rural and nonrural counties.
- Nonrural counties shifted further right, resulting in a wider rural-nonrural gap by 2023.
- The density overlap in 2018 was near-complete; by 2023, nonrural counties show a distinctly higher mode.
- Outliers removed by IQR method to focus on the typical county rather than extreme cases.

## Reproducibility

Generated by `R/viz/presentation/remote_work_density_plot.R` in the producing project.
